home.available: Available for web products, full-stack systems, and automation
home.name: Evyatar Hazan
home.subtitle: Full Stack Developer turning business needs into stable digital products
home.description: I build product websites, admin systems, and practical tools end to end: clear UX, polished frontends, structured backends, and validation workflows that make future development safer.
home.whatsappText: Hi Evyatar, I saw your website and would like to talk about a project.

compiler.prelude: An idea is only the beginning.
compiler.headline: I turn complexity into a product that works.
compiler.description: Business need, user experience, and engineering — connected into one clear, maintainable product.

repos.title: 7 selected projects
repos.description: Live websites, monorepos, and public tools across React, TypeScript, backend, and mobile.

delivery.title: Structured delivery
delivery.description: Short discovery, phased implementation, validation, deployment, and documentation for future maintenance.

systems.title: Broad product range
systems.description: Business websites, admin systems, APIs, Prisma, OAuth, Cloudinary, and React Native.

frame.title: Need
frame.description: Clarify the problem and the outcome.

prove.title: Proof
prove.description: Validate, deploy, and verify the live result.

about.summary: A good product does not end with code. It should be clear to users, maintainable for teams, and proven in the live version.

projects.subtitle: Four products built around real needs. Each one exposes the decision, the system, and the proof — not only the final screen.
projects.caseStudyNotFoundDescription: This project does not have a published case study page yet.
projects.caseStudyNotFoundSeoTitle: Case study not found | Evyatar Hazan
projects.indexSubtitle: Three more projects that broaden the picture without repeating the four proof stories above.

nis_boutique.title: Nis Boutique Catering
nis_boutique.description: A conversion-focused boutique catering website from Beitar Illit, built around WhatsApp, SEO, and RTL UX.
nis_boutique.summary: A live business website for a boutique catering brand, guiding visitors from first impression to a WhatsApp conversation.
nis_boutique.impact: The site brings brand positioning, service categories, gallery, FAQ, and one clear CTA into a shareable marketing asset.
nis_boutique.previewTitle: Live preview of the Nis Boutique Catering website
nis_boutique.problem: A local service business needed a site that communicates quality, earns trust, and drives inquiries without relying on pricing or heavy content.
nis_boutique.solution: A Hebrew landing page with clear messaging hierarchy, service categories, gallery, FAQ, persistent CTA, and custom-domain deployment.
nis_boutique.role: Discovery, copy, frontend design, motion, SEO, baseline accessibility, and Vercel deployment.

online_converter.title: Online Converter
online_converter.description: A client-side conversion tool for JSON, CSV, XML, and YAML.
online_converter.summary: A browser tool for converting common data formats without sending content to an external server.
online_converter.impact: Useful for sensitive data or technical drafts, with a short workflow for validation, copying, and downloading.
online_converter.problem: Developers and technical users need fast data conversion without sending sensitive content to an external server.
online_converter.solution: A React and TypeScript interface with i18n, client-side validation, copy, download, and a short task flow.
online_converter.role: Product planning, frontend architecture, interaction flow, and CI scripts.

emergency_protocol.title: Emergency Protocol Diagram
emergency_protocol.description: A full-stack system for presenting, managing, and discussing emergency protocols.
emergency_protocol.summary: A system that turns complex emergency protocols into an interactive workspace with users, auth, and comments.
emergency_protocol.impact: The project shows how a static document can become a structured product with permissions, database models, and maintainable delivery.
emergency_protocol.previewTitle: Live preview of Emergency Protocol Diagram
emergency_protocol.problem: Complex protocols are hard to understand, update, and share across stakeholders.
emergency_protocol.solution: A React client, Express API, Prisma, Google OAuth, comments, and a structured validation flow.
emergency_protocol.role: Monorepo structure, API, authentication, user interface, and test infrastructure.

lev_chedva.title: Lev Hedva
lev_chedva.description: Web and API infrastructure for a nonprofit, including website, client app, and server.
lev_chedva.summary: A nonprofit monorepo separating the public site, client app, and backend API.
lev_chedva.impact: The structure supports multiple interfaces around one product while keeping scripts, responsibilities, and code boundaries clear.
lev_chedva.problem: A nonprofit digital property needs to be clear, maintainable, and ready to grow.
lev_chedva.solution: A separated website, client app, and backend with shared infrastructure and consistent scripts.
lev_chedva.role: Repository organization, frontend layer, API, and day-to-day developer workflows.

password_gen.title: Password Generator
password_gen.description: A privacy-first password generator with a React Native app and reusable core package.
password_gen.summary: A mobile app and reusable core package for generating passwords locally on-device.
password_gen.impact: The project keeps a basic security workflow private and reusable instead of tying it to an external service.
password_gen.problem: A basic security tool must be simple, local, and independent of external services.
password_gen.solution: A reusable core package and mobile app that generate passwords locally on the device.
password_gen.role: Core logic, package structure, React Native, and privacy-led product decisions.

test_yourself.title: Test Yourself
test_yourself.description: A practice and quiz platform with a React client and Express API.
test_yourself.summary: A learning platform foundation with client, API, validation scripts, and monorepo structure.
test_yourself.impact: Built for educational content that needs to grow gradually without breaking existing quizzes, exercises, or flows.
test_yourself.problem: Practice systems need to be easy to extend and validate without breaking existing content.
test_yourself.solution: A TypeScript monorepo with client, API, validation scripts, and CI.
test_yourself.role: Product structure, frontend, backend, and testing flow.

united_hatzalah.title: United Hatzalah Shoham Branch
united_hatzalah.description: A nonprofit branch website and admin interface with React, Express, Prisma, and Cloudinary.
united_hatzalah.summary: A full-stack system for a local branch website, content management, media, and operational data.
united_hatzalah.impact: The project reduces reliance on code changes for every update by introducing content, media, and public display workflows.
united_hatzalah.problem: A local branch needs a reliable website and admin workflow without code changes for every update.
united_hatzalah.solution: A full-stack system with media management, database models, API, and structured deployment.
united_hatzalah.role: Frontend, backend, data models, media uploads, and CI workflows.

contact.subtitle: A need, an idea, or a system that is stuck? Send the context. We will work out what is worth building — and whether it should be built.
contact.statusDesc: For projects, collaborations, and technical thinking
contact.formTitle: Three lines are enough to begin.
contact.getInTouchDesc: Send a few lines about the need, the current state, and what should happen next. If it fits, we can move into a short scope and work plan.
contact.whatsappText: Hi Evyatar, I saw your website and would like to talk about a project.

form.msgPh: Tell me briefly what you want to build...
form.successMsg: Message sent successfully. I will get back to you soon.
form.errorMsg: Message delivery failed. You can reach out directly on WhatsApp or email.
form.privacyNotice: Form submissions are processed by FormSubmit and forwarded by email.

blog.subtitle: Short notes about building products, frontend systems, live websites, validation, deployment, and decisions that keep working after launch.
blog.notFoundDescription: The link may have changed, or this post may not exist in the current language yet.

seo.indexDescription: Short articles about product engineering, frontend work, live websites, validation, and deployment.

blogPreview.subtitle: An open working log about the systems, decisions, and lessons behind the finished product.
